my_thoughts: The definition works: super-effort = external intentionality/obedience (not internal intention), criterion (external vs. internal). The student asks for definition (seeking technical definition), which shows they're seeking technical definition. They'll likely ask about distinction next. Hypothesis: technical definitions trigger distinction questions—testable by tracking whether technical definitions lead to distinction inquiries.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student asks for definition ('What is a super-effort?')—seeking technical definition via asks for definition and seeks conceptual clarity. They're seeking technical definition. The comparison_contrast mechanism defines: super-effort = external intentionality/obedience (not internal intention), criterion (external vs. internal). Pattern: definition question → definition → [next: distinction question]. Low confidence because definitions can lead to clarification requests or deeper questions.
visible_response:
A super-effort can have many characteristics, but generally it is doing not what you
think best, but what you are advised to do.
From our own intentions we are ready to do
many things, but we are not ready to do or not to do what we are told.
